briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — steady summer approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for summer with clear water and stable conditions.

Robertson: When postspawners leave the tapering points, follow them with a Berkley Dredger 20.5 or 25.5 down ditches and drainages 10-15 feet deep that lead from spawning flats toward the main lake.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When postspawners leave the tapering points, follow them with a Berkley Dredger 20.5 or 25.5 down ditches and drainages 10-15 feet deep that lead from spawning flats toward the main lake.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-p
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Casting angles are critical for deep cranking offshore structure - varying your angle of retrieve relative to the cover triggers reaction strikes from bigger fish.
Palaniuk: 'Deep diving crankbaits are really one of the few ways we have of generating any kind of reaction strike through much of the year on tour.'

KVD: At Toledo Bend, started each morning shallow on the shad spawn for 1-1.5 hours, then transitioned offshore to deep crank. The biggest fish all came deep cranking with 6XD, 8XD, 10XD. Didn't get a ton of bites but when he got bit, it was a giant.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
